mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings downgrades, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real personal savings and housing starts indicates robust consumer balance sheets and durable demand, which jointly expand corporate revenue forecasts and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series rise simultaneously, or single-series Rank IC exceeds the interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals productive capacity outpacing demand constraints, which improves corporate margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly reflects aggregate economic activity that drives corporate revenue expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth remains statistically indistinguishable from zero across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 4.5 percent impose restrictive financing conditions that suppress equity valuations and corporate investment, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets deliver positive 60-day returns in more than half of all observations where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5 percent for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and unemployment rate elevation indicate a synchronized labor market cooling that accelerates earnings revision cycles downward, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal does not outperform single-series baselines in predicting negative 20-day returns during peak cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Post-shock recovery phases characterized by rising real GDP and stabilizing inflation compress risk premiums, which immediately lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain flat or negative when GDPC1 expands and CPIAUCSL stabilizes within the defined post-shock window.
